## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi, this is Don Crowther with a video about using retargeting ads to get people to attend your webinar. So you're already a pro, so you already know this, but just in case a retargeting ad is an ad that you see as a result of having visited a given web page in the past. So for example, I visit a page to buy some faded jeans, but I don't buy, and then I start seeing an ads to buy those same faded jeans on Facebook and ESPN, New York Times, and many other sites, and I go, how in the world do they do that? Well, it's a strategy called retargeting or Google calls it remarketing. Basically, what you do is you set up an account, you put a few lines of code on your pages, and then what you're able to actually do is to dictate the people who see a certain page. And sometimes you can have the people who see page A but don't see page B. So for example, people who saw my sales page but didn't see my thank you page after they bought, then shown this ad. So then you create an ad campaign, and then they show your ads to people to meet certain criteria. So you can use people who saw this page but didn't see that page, or you could use people who saw this page, but didn't see that page who are between the ages of 30 and 45, or who live in Boston. So there's lots of different ways that you can use this criteria to be able to really target those ads down to exactly the people who really should have bought or in this case attended your webinar. So what we'll do here is that we're going to retarget only people who have been to my registrations thank you page. So I'm not going to retarget people for this particular strategy who came to my registration page but didn't actually sign up. I'm only going to show these ads to people who actually signed up. Okay, then I'm going to set up ads pointing people at my asset pages. And those ads are only running between signing up and the webinar. So I'm going to have an ad going to this transformational copy page and add going to my trust builder page and add going to my personal personalization promise page. So I've got different ads showing to different pages from my asset set. And remember the only people who are seeing this are people who actually signed up for the webinar. So I don't have to pay a lot of money for these ads. So I recommend that you do this first on Facebook.

## [03:01 - 05:33]

Drop dead easy, drop a certain piece of code in just works. Google it. It'll show you exactly how to do it. My job is not teach how to do retargeting here. And then advanced. And you'll see there are a number of them out there more than one handful, two handfuls, maybe three handfuls of companies that do this. And most of them have arrangements with a large group of companies. So things like CNN and BBC and Forbes and LinkedIn and Instagram and ESPN and of course Facebook and all kinds of different places. I have had many times where I've had people come up to me and say how much you spending on your ad campaign. I see your ads everywhere. And it's like I just sort of laughed myself because I realize I probably spent less than $2,000. So the first time I use this in a launch, I ran about 23 $2,500 worth of ads and got about $45,000 worth of purchases from it. Incredibly powerful. So strategy. What are we doing here? We're staying in front of our registrants. We're reminding them frequently of this webinar that they've signed up for. And in the process of that, I'm driving them to the other assets I've created in my post registration asset set. Literally because there's so few people who are actually signing up and Facebook isn't going to show your ads to every single person who signed up every single day. $200 is probably 75% exaggeration here. You're probably going to spend 50 bucks on this strategy. So it is one of those things that is in so incredibly leveraged huge results, small spending, strongly recommend that you do this. Even if all you do is just keep reminding them of the webinar and don't even send them to the other asset pages. It'll be better than nothing.
